content:
# Source Rock Evaluation

**Summary**: The assessment of the potential of sedimentary rocks to generate hydrocarbons.

---

## How It Appears in the Thesis Collection

- [[2012-ghazwani|Ghazwani, 2012]] focused on source-rock-evaluation for the Hanadir member.

- [[2016-abbas|Abbas, 2016]] evaluated the source potential of the shale interbeds using TOC analysis.

- [[2017-mohammed|Mohammed, 2017]] performed source-rock-evaluation on the TQMN formation.

- [[2017-abdelgader|Abdelgader, 2017]] evaluated the Abu Gabra Formation as a primary source rock.

- [[2020-usman|Usman, 2020]] evaluated the source-rock potential of carbonate mudrocks in the Jafurah sub-basin.

- [[2022-septriandi|Septriandi, 2022]] evaluated the source-rock quality of the Jubaila Formation using TOC, S, and Fe data.

- [[2023-al-eid|Al-Eid, 2023]] evaluated source rock using pyrolysis parameters S1, S2, and TMAX.

- [[2025-alhubail|Alhubail, 2025]] performed source-rock-evaluation on carbonate mudrocks to assess their interaction with CO2.

## Key Subtopics

- [[2012-ghazwani|Ghazwani, 2012]] TOC, HI, Tmax, vitrinite reflectance, palynology.

- [[2016-abbas|Abbas, 2016]] evaluated the source potential of the shale interbeds using TOC analysis.

- [[2017-mohammed|Mohammed, 2017]] performed source-rock-evaluation on the TQMN formation.

- [[2017-abdelgader|Abdelgader, 2017]] evaluated the Abu Gabra Formation as a primary source rock.

- [[2020-usman|Usman, 2020]] evaluated the source-rock potential of carbonate mudrocks in the Jafurah sub-basin.

- [[2022-septriandi|Septriandi, 2022]] evaluated the source-rock quality of the Jubaila Formation using TOC, S, and Fe data.

- [[2023-al-eid|Al-Eid, 2023]] evaluated source rock using pyrolysis parameters S1, S2, and TMAX.

- [[2025-alhubail|Alhubail, 2025]] performed source-rock-evaluation on carbonate mudrocks to assess their interaction with CO2.

## Methods and Approaches Used

- [[2012-ghazwani|Ghazwani, 2012]] Rock-Eval pyrolysis, micropaleontology.

## Key Findings Across Sources

- Hanadir is a candidate for being a main source of gas in Northwest Saudi Arabia. ([[2012-ghazwani|Ghazwani, 2012, p. 15]])

- TOC was determined to acquire a generalized trend for the source rock potential of the shale interbeds. ([[2016-abbas|Abbas, 2016, p. 55]])

- TQMN was identified as a high-quality source rock with significant gas potential. ([[2017-mohammed|Mohammed, 2017, p. 130]])

- Source rock for this hydrocarbon is believed to be the lacustrine shale of the Abu Gabra Formation. ([[2017-abdelgader|Abdelgader, 2017, p. 64]])

- Two lithofacies were found to have very good to excellent hydrocarbon generation potential. ([[2020-usman|Usman, 2020, p. 17]])

- TOC levels were correlated with sulfur and iron content to assess the maturity and potential of the formation. ([[2022-septriandi|Septriandi, 2022, p. 117]])

- Relying on unrepresentative measurements from poorly preserved samples can critically impact results used for source rock assessment. ([[2023-al-eid|Al-Eid, 2023, p. 17]])

- Carbonate mudrocks were evaluated as potential source rocks in the context of CO2 interactions. ([[2025-alhubail|Alhubail, 2025, p. 5]])

## Competing Approaches and Debates

- [[2012-ghazwani|Ghazwani, 2012]] Determining if the Han1ir member is a viable source rock for gas.
